question: According to Walmart's U.S. representation data on page 14, what percentage of the U.S. workforce and U.S. management are women and people of color?
answers: U.S. workforce: 52% women, 49% people of color. U.S. management: 44% women, 41% people of color. U.S. officers: 37% women, 28% people of color. U.S. total management promotions earned: 40% women, 43% people of color. U.S. hourly-to-hourly promotions earned: 54% women, 50% people of color.
Workforce: 52% women, 49% POC. Management: 44% women, 41% POC. Officers: 37% women, 28% POC. Management promotions: 40% women, 43% POC.

question: What is Walmart's 'Project Gigaton' and what is its target? What are the six action areas that suppliers are encouraged to take action in?
answers: Project Gigaton is an ambitious initiative launched by Walmart in 2017 to catalyze decarbonization in product value chains through engagement of suppliers, NGOs, and other stakeholders. The goal is to help reduce or avoid one billion metric tons (a 'gigaton') of greenhouse gas emissions by 2030. The six action areas are: Energy, Packaging, Nature, Transportation, Waste, and Product Use & Design.

Project Gigaton (launched 2017): reduce/avoid 1 billion metric tons of GHG emissions by 2030 across product value chains. Six areas: Energy, Packaging, Nature, Transportation, Waste, Product Use & Design.

question: What progress did Walmart make in FY2023 toward its zero waste goal? Include metrics on food waste reduction, packaging recyclability, and plastic waste elimination.
answers: FY2023 waste progress: Reduced food waste by 12% versus 2016 baseline; diverted more than 900 million lbs of food waste to composting, animal feed, anaerobic digestion, and biochemical processing; donated more than 760 million lbs of food globally. 63% of global private-brand packaging is recyclable, reusable, or industrially compostable, a 5% increase over FY2022. Walmart also eliminated free single-use plastic bags across several markets.

Food waste: 12% reduction vs 2016 baseline, 900M+ lbs diverted, 760M+ lbs donated. Packaging: 63% recyclable/reusable/compostable (+5% vs FY2022). Plastic: eliminated free single-use bags in several markets.

question: According to Walmart's Corporate Governance section, what are the Board independence and diversity metrics, and on which page are these reported? Also, what are the nine core program foundations of the Ethics & Compliance program?
answers: Board governance metrics on page 33: Director independence — 67% independent Board members (FY2023), 64% in FY2022 and FY2021. Women on Board: 25% in FY2023 (27% in FY2022 and FY2021). Ethnically/racially diverse Board members: 17% in FY2023 (18% in FY2022 and FY2021). The Ethics & Compliance program was updated to focus on nine core program foundations (page 32).
Page 33: Board independence — 67% independent; Women — 25%; Racially/ethnically diverse — 17%. Page 32: Ethics & Compliance program updated with 9 core program foundations.

question: Describe Walmart's nature conservation targets: what are the acreage and ocean area goals by 2030, and what progress had been made through the Acres for America program by FY2023?
answers: Walmart aims to protect, more sustainably manage, or restore at least 50 million acres of land and 1 million square miles of ocean by 2030. Through the Acres for America program, Walmart reached a milestone of over 2 million acres conserved since 2005. In FY2023, Walmart engaged through business or philanthropy in efforts to protect, more sustainably manage, or restore more than 30 million acres of land and more than 1.4 million square miles of ocean. The company also pursued seven place-based commodity sourcing initiatives and launched a nature portal to connect suppliers to conservation opportunities.

2030 targets: 50 million acres of land + 1 million sq miles of ocean. Acres for America: 2M+ acres conserved since 2005. FY2023: >30M acres land + >1.4M sq miles ocean engaged. 7 place-based sourcing initiatives launched.

question: Walmart's ESG strategy is built on four pillars: Opportunity, Sustainability, Community, and Ethics & Integrity. For the Opportunity pillar, what were the key FY2023 metrics regarding associate compensation, internal promotions, and diverse supplier spending?
answers: Key FY2023 metrics for Opportunity: $21.75 U.S. associate average total hourly compensation; 68% full-time U.S. hourly associates; over 34,000 associates enrolled in Live Better U; $47 billion cumulative total purchases supporting American jobs (2021 through FY2023); more than 180,000 U.S. associates received promotions with 88% of roles above entry level filled internally; and more than $13 billion sourced from approximately 2,400 diverse suppliers to U.S. businesses.
$21.75 avg hourly total compensation; 68% full-time; 34,000+ in Live Better U; $47B supporting American jobs; 180,000+ promotions (88% internal fill rate); $13B+ from ~2,400 diverse suppliers.

question: What is Walmart's climate target for its global operations emissions, and what was the reduction in combined Scope 1 & 2 emissions versus the 2015 baseline as reported in FY2023?
answers: Walmart aims to achieve zero emissions in its global operations by 2040. As of FY2023 (through 2021 data), Walmart achieved a 23.2% reduction in combined Scope 1 & 2 emissions versus the 2015 baseline. Through Project Gigaton, suppliers have reduced or avoided more than 750 million metric tons of CO2e since 2017.
Zero emissions in global operations by 2040. Combined Scope 1&2 emissions reduced by 23.2% vs 2015 baseline (through 2021). Suppliers reduced/avoided 750M+ MT CO2e since 2017 via Project Gigaton.

question: Walmart's ESG strategy covers four pillars: Opportunity, Sustainability, Community, and Ethics & Integrity. Synthesize the headline aspiration key metric, and one specific FY2023 highlight for each pillar.
answers: Opportunity: Making retail a place of inclusive opportunity. Key metric: $21.75 average total hourly compensation, 180,000+ promotions (88% internal fill). Highlight: 34,000+ associates enrolled in Live Better U (page 11-12). Sustainability: Zero emissions by 2040 and 1 billion metric tons reduction through Project Gigaton by 2030. Key metric: 23.2% Scope 1&2 reduction vs 2015, 750M+ MT CO2e reduced by suppliers since 2017. Highlight: 63% packaging recyclable (page 17, 19, 22). Community: Helping communities thrive. Key metric: >665 million lbs food donated in U.S., >$1.7 billion cash and in-kind donations globally. Highlight: Launched Walmart Healthcare Research Institute (page 25). Ethics & Integrity: Making trust a competitive advantage. Key metric: >1.4 million associates completed Code of Conduct training, 67% independent Board. Highlight: Updated Ethics & Compliance program to nine core foundations (page 31-33).

Four pillars: (1) Opportunity — $21.75/hr, 180K promotions, 34K Live Better U; (2) Sustainability — zero emissions by 2040, 750M MT reduced, 63% packaging recyclable; (3) Community — 665M lbs food, $1.7B donations, Healthcare Research Institute; (4) Ethics — 1.4M Code of Conduct trained, 67% independent Board, 9 core compliance foundations.
